bibimbap.title: Bibimbap
bibimbap.subtitle: A mixed rice bowl completed with colorful namul toppings
paragraphs: Bibimbap is one of Korea's signature rice bowls, served with assorted namul, meat, egg, gochujang, and sesame oil.
Each topping is prepared separately so its color, aroma, and texture remain distinct before everything is mixed together.
When paired with a craft feature, users can place namul on the rice themselves and experience how arrangement and mixing complete the dish.

info.ingredients: Rice · Namul · Gochujang · Sesame oil · Egg
info.occasion: Rice bowl meal · Interactive menu · Korean food introduction
info.craft: Choose namul · Arrange toppings · Complete your own bibimbap

bom_namul.title: Spring Namul
bom_namul.subtitle: Fresh spring greens lightly seasoned for the new season
paragraphs: Spring namul highlights young greens such as wild chives, shepherd's purse, and shoots gathered as the season begins.
Light seasoning with soy sauce, doenjang, and sesame oil keeps the herbs fragrant and crisp.
It brings a clean, lightly bitter freshness to the table and helps wake the appetite after winter.

info.ingredients: Wild chives · Shepherd's purse · Shoots · Sesame oil · Doenjang
info.occasion: Spring meals · Light side dish · Appetite starter
info.pairing: Steamed rice · Doenjang soup · Assorted namul

hwajeon.title: Hwajeon
hwajeon.subtitle: Flower-topped rice cakes pan-fried for spring tea tables
paragraphs: Hwajeon is made by pressing edible flower petals onto small rounds of glutinous rice dough.
The chewy texture and bright petals make it one of the most visual foods of spring.
It is often served with tea as a delicate seasonal snack.

info.ingredients: Glutinous rice flour · Edible flowers · Honey · Oil
info.occasion: Spring tea time · Picnic · Traditional snack

ssukguk.title: Mugwort Soup
ssukguk.subtitle: A clear spring soup scented with young mugwort
paragraphs: Ssukguk is a warm soup made with tender mugwort, an herb strongly associated with spring in Korea.
A clear broth or light doenjang base lets the green aroma come forward without heaviness.
It fits naturally on a home table during the season's change.

strawberry_dessert.title: Strawberry Dessert
strawberry_dessert.subtitle: A light dessert built around bright spring berries
paragraphs: Spring strawberries bring vivid sweetness and acidity to simple desserts.
Cream, milk, or yogurt softens the fruit's brightness while keeping the finish fresh.
It is an easy way to close a meal with a clear sense of the season.

info.pairing: Black tea · Yogurt · Vanilla ice cream

naengmyeon.title: Naengmyeon
naengmyeon.subtitle: Cold broth and buckwheat noodles for summer heat
paragraphs: Naengmyeon is a chilled noodle dish built around icy broth and springy noodles.
Dongchimi or beef broth gives it a clean savory base, sharpened with vinegar and mustard.
It is light enough for hot days while still feeling like a complete meal.

info.ingredients: Buckwheat noodles · Broth · Dongchimi · Cucumber · Egg
info.occasion: Summer lunch · Cooling down · Eating out

samgyetang.title: Samgyetang
samgyetang.subtitle: Ginseng chicken soup served as a summer restorative
paragraphs: Samgyetang is made by simmering a young chicken with glutinous rice, ginseng, jujube, and garlic.
The broth is clear yet deep, and the tender chicken makes the dish deeply satisfying.
It is closely associated with Korea's hottest summer days as a restorative meal.

info.ingredients: Chicken · Ginseng · Glutinous rice · Jujube · Garlic

kongguksu.title: Kongguksu
kongguksu.subtitle: Noodles in chilled, nutty soybean broth
paragraphs: Kongguksu is a summer noodle dish served in a cold broth made from finely ground soybeans.
The soybean broth is creamy and nutty without being heavy.
Regional preferences for salt or sugar make the dish flexible and personal.

info.occasion: Summer meal · Vegetarian bowl · Cold noodles
info.pairing: Young radish kimchi · Cucumber salad · Fresh geotjeori

watermelon_hwachae.title: Watermelon Hwachae
watermelon_hwachae.subtitle: A chilled fruit punch for summer desserts
paragraphs: Watermelon hwachae combines watermelon and other fruits in a cold, sweet drink.
The juicy fruit and chilled liquid make it especially refreshing in hot weather.
It is simple to prepare and bright enough for summer gatherings.

info.pairing: Rice cakes · Hangwa · Light tea snacks

songpyeon.title: Songpyeon
songpyeon.subtitle: Festive rice cakes made with new harvest grains
paragraphs: Songpyeon is a half-moon rice cake filled with sesame, beans, chestnuts, or other sweet fillings.
Steaming it over pine needles gives the cakes a gentle forest aroma.
It is one of the symbolic foods of Chuseok, often made and shared by families.

info.ingredients: Rice flour · Sesame · Beans · Chestnuts · Pine needles

grilled_gizzard_shad.title: Grilled Gizzard Shad
grilled_gizzard_shad.subtitle: An autumn fish prized for its rich, oily flavor
paragraphs: Gizzard shad becomes especially flavorful in autumn when the fish is rich with oil.
A simple salt grill makes the skin crisp and keeps the flesh savory.
It is a seasonal favorite where the ingredient itself carries the dish.

info.ingredients: Gizzard shad · Coarse salt · Lemon · Radish
info.occasion: Autumn specialty · Drinks table · Seasonal fish

mushroom_jeongol.title: Mushroom Jeongol
mushroom_jeongol.subtitle: A warm hot pot centered on seasonal mushrooms
paragraphs: Mushroom jeongol simmers several mushrooms, vegetables, and broth together at the table.
The mushrooms lend aroma and umami to the broth without making it heavy.
It is well suited to cool autumn evenings and shared meals.

info.ingredients: Shiitake · Oyster mushrooms · Enoki · Vegetables · Broth
info.occasion: Autumn dinner · Shared meal · Hot pot

chestnut_rice.title: Chestnut Rice
chestnut_rice.subtitle: Autumn rice cooked with sweet chestnuts
paragraphs: Chestnut rice adds freshly harvested chestnuts to steamed rice for a gentle autumn sweetness.
The chestnuts become soft and fragrant, making the rice itself feel seasonal.
It turns a simple home table into a harvest meal.

info.occasion: Autumn home meal · Harvest table · Lunchbox

seolleongtang.title: Seolleongtang
seolleongtang.subtitle: Long-simmered ox bone soup for cold days
paragraphs: Seolleongtang is made by simmering ox bones and beef until the broth turns milky and mild.
Seasoning at the table with salt and scallions keeps the flavor clean.
It is a comforting winter bowl that warms the body and fills the table.

info.ingredients: Ox bones · Beef brisket · Noodles · Scallions · Salt
info.occasion: Winter meal · Hangover soup · Hearty bowl

tteokguk.title: Tteokguk
tteokguk.subtitle: Rice cake soup traditionally eaten for the new year
paragraphs: Tteokguk is made with sliced garaetteok simmered in a clear, savory broth.
Soft rice cakes, broth, and colorful garnishes make it both simple and ceremonial.
It marks the beginning of the year and is strongly tied to family gatherings.

info.ingredients: Rice cakes · Beef broth · Egg · Scallions · Gim
info.occasion: Seollal · New year meal · Family table

kimchi_jjigae.title: Kimchi Jjigae
kimchi_jjigae.subtitle: A warm stew built from deeply fermented kimchi
paragraphs: Kimchi jjigae is a classic home stew made with aged kimchi, pork, tofu, and aromatics.
The kimchi's acidity and spice create a deep broth that pairs naturally with rice.
It is familiar, warming, and welcome in both winter and everyday meals.

info.ingredients: Aged kimchi · Pork · Tofu · Scallions · Gochugaru
info.occasion: Home meal · Winter dinner · Rice companion

hotteok.title: Hotteok
hotteok.subtitle: A sweet filled pancake from winter street stalls
paragraphs: Hotteok is a pan-fried dough filled with brown sugar, cinnamon, and nuts.
The outside browns while the filling melts into a hot syrup.
It is a handheld winter snack closely tied to street food memories.
